blocks.content: Outside, the lamp post was doing its ordinary thing, which included the carnival poster that caught the light at certain angles. Maya hadn't been reading it — she'd been looking past it — but Nathan turned to see what had drawn her eye.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: It's been up for a few days.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: I might go. I haven't been to one since I was fifteen. Somehow they always seemed like things I'd do and then I'd never actually do them.
He was quiet for a moment.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: What's the last thing you did that you'd been meaning to do for a long time?
Maya looked at him. There was something in the question that was different from the ones before it — less performance, more genuine curiosity.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: I came here tonight. I'd been putting it off.
He took that in without making anything of it.

blocks.speaker: Nathan
blocks.content: I know you're here for your sister. I know you're paying attention to everything, even when you look like you're not. I know you answered a geography question that stopped the table cold and didn't say anything about it after.
Maya looked at him for a moment that was slightly longer than the one before it.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: You're better at this than I expected.
